target.title: feat(qa): crabline channel driver
target.body:
## Summary

- Wires profile-selected `channelDriver: crabline` into QA suite execution instead of only writing Crabline metadata artifacts.
- Adds a Crabline-backed Telegram QA transport that sends scenario inbound text through Crabline, configures a real OpenClaw Telegram gateway account for SUT replies, and mirrors observed replies back into the existing QA Lab bus.
- Reuses the existing QA credential lease path so Crabline Telegram can read direct `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_*` env locally or lease Telegram credentials from Convex in CI with `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=convex`.
- Adds `qa run --qa-profile ... --scenario ...` filtering so CI can run a small taxonomy/profile-owned smoke slice while still using the profile's `channelDriver`.
- Adds a secret-bearing workflow lane that runs the `smoke-ci` profile through Crabline with Convex credential leases.
- Pins QA Lab's dev-only Crabline dependency to merged `openclaw/crabline@ee1189ad966e9e8d7eea9e38b3ca5965ff48d93c` and lazy-loads it so production OpenClaw dist does not bundle Crabline provider code.

## Linked context

- Uses merged openclaw/crabline#9, which exports the runtime registry/provider API from Crabline's package root.
- Builds on the earlier profile/taxonomy work in this PR: `smoke-ci` selects `crabline`, `release` declares `live`, and default profiles continue to use `qa-channel`.
- Related follow-up openclaw/openclaw#91506 preserves suite selections across the isolated runner wrapper.

## CI credential setup

- CI does not need raw `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_GROUP_ID`, `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN`, or `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_SUT_BOT_TOKEN` secrets for this lane.
- The new `run_smoke_ci_crabline` job in `qa-live-transports-convex.yml` uses the existing `qa-live-shared` secrets: `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SITE_URL` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_CI`.
- The job sets `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=convex` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_ROLE=ci`, then runs `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --provider-mode mock-openai --fast`.

## Real behavior proof

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts` passes 141/141 tests, including coverage for importing the installed Crabline package and acquiring Telegram credentials through the Convex broker path.
- `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/crabline-no-env-check --provider-mode mock-openai --fast` resolves `smoke-ci` to one scenario and fails locally at the direct-env fallback with `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN is required for --channel-driver crabline telegram.` That proves the taxonomy profile enters Crabline; CI satisfies this with the Convex credential source.
- A full live Telegram smoke run was not possible in this shell because the Telegram credentials are intentionally brokered through CI secrets.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/qa-lab/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/crabline-no-env-check --provider-mode mock-openai --fast` expectedly fails locally at missing direct Telegram QA env.
- `git diff --check`

## Production bundle/dependency check

- `crabline` appears only in `extensions/qa-lab` `devDependencies`, not `dependencies` or `peerDependencies`.
- Previous clean build verification on this branch showed `rg "node_modules/crabline|providers/builtin" dist -n` returns no matches.
- Built `dist` still contains expected QA strings like `channel-driver crabline`, but not Crabline provider/runtime chunks.

## Current limits

- QA Lab's Crabline execution transport currently supports Telegram only. Non-Telegram Crabline selections fail explicitly.
- Generic message actions like reaction/edit/delete are not implemented through the Crabline Telegram transport yet.
- Telegram direct QA conversations are represented through the configured Telegram QA group because Telegram bots cannot DM each other.

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

target.title: feat(doctor): add doctor --explain diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw doctor --explain` for plain-English structured doctor findings.
- Keeps explain mode read-only: no prompts, no config/state writes, no repair hook calls.
- Keeps default `openclaw doctor`, `doctor --fix`, and `doctor --lint --json` behavior unchanged.
- Keeps `--only`/`--skip` scoped to read-only `--lint` and `--explain`; the earlier per-check repair selector was removed from this PR and should be a follow-up only if maintainers explicitly approve that public CLI contract.

Refs #94241

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Adds read-only plain-English explain output for structured doctor findings without adding a new mutating repair command or changing default doctor/lint/fix behavior.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, branch `codex/doctor-explain`, commit `5cc49a92`, Node `v23.6.1`, isolated `.artifacts/doctor-explain-proof` `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  rm -rf .artifacts/doctor-explain-proof
  mkdir -p .artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/home
  HOME="$PWD/.artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/home" \
    OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PWD/.artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/state" \
    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$PWD/.artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/openclaw.json" \
    node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --explain --only core/doctor/gateway-config \
    > .artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/output.txt 2>&1
  find .artifacts/doctor-explain-proof -maxdepth 2 -type f -print | sort
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  doctor --explain: ran 1 check(s), 1 finding(s)

  Gateway config [warning]
    Check: core/doctor/gateway-config
    What happened: Gateway setup is incomplete.
    Why it matters: OpenClaw cannot reliably start or find the Gateway until this is configured.
    Try this: Run `openclaw configure` and set Gateway mode (local/remote), or `openclaw config set gateway.mode local`.
    Automatic repair: This report is read-only. Run openclaw doctor --fix to apply supported repairs.
    Details:
    - gateway.mode: gateway.mode is unset; gateway start will be blocked.

  files_after_probe:
  .artifacts/doctor-explain-proof/output.txt
  ```
- Observed result after fix: command exited `1` because the selected warning-level finding was present; explain output rendered the new plain-English sections; no `openclaw.json` was written in the isolated proof directory.
- What was not tested: cross-platform/package install smoke, broad `pnpm check:changed`, and full CI locally. Interactive per-check repair is intentionally not tested here because this PR no longer adds an explain prompt or repair selector behavior.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts` (22 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-explain.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts docs/cli/doctor.md docs/gateway/doctor.md`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg "repairableCheckIds|runDoctorSelectedRepairCli|explainRepairPromptLabel|confirmRepairCheck" src docs` (no matches)
- real CLI proof above with isolated OpenClaw home/config/state

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, additive CLI output behind `--explain`.

Did config, environment, migration, or repair behavior change? No. This update removes the new per-check repair path from the PR; explain mode is read-only, and existing `doctor --fix` remains the only repair posture touched by users.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No new secret, network, or tool execution behavior. Existing `--allow-exec` gating is only passed through to read-only structured checks.

Compatibility posture: conservative additive flag, no default output change, no new mutating command path, no config/default surface, no migration behavior, and docs now match the code.

## Current review state

AI-assisted PR. I understand the code changes and verified the behavior above. Latest update specifically addresses the compatibility-risk feedback by splitting out the per-check repair selector and leaving it for a future issue/PR only after maintainer approval.

target.title: feat(agent-sdk): add packaging spec + skeleton
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the Agent SDK Packaging spec (v0.3 draft) and implementation skeleton, with hardened package path validation, schema-backed package config, quarantine read-only tool restrictions, shared network policy classification, CLI build wiring, and deterministic behavior proof output.

## What's Included

- `docs/specs/agent-sdk-packaging.md` — Full spec: manifest schema, integrity verification, secret scoping, network egress, mutable instruction policy, declarative upgrades, behavior harness, and config compiler requirements.
- `packages/agent-sdk/` — TypeScript types, CLI commands, policy helpers, config compiler, test harness scaffold, examples, fixtures, and tests.
- Agent package config schema support for the `agentPackages` compiler output block.

## Key Design Decisions

- **openclaw.integrity.json** (not lockfile) — content integrity, not dependency resolution.
- **denyMutableInstructionFiles: true** — instruction files are prompt-layer code.
- **Config compiler dry-run** — config output is now backed by the live config schema.
- **No scripts, no hooks** — purely declarative upgrades.
- **Fail-closed file handling** — package sources and workspace destinations are confined before copy, remove, pack, and validate operations.

## Status

Draft PR. Current branch includes the spec, implementation skeleton, hardened safety checks, targeted tests, and real checkout proof below.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent SDK package workflow builds CLI entrypoints, validates package-owned files before copy/remove/hash operations, keeps quarantine tool access read-only, uses shared network special-use IP classification, validates emitted `agentPackages` config through schema support. The CLI `test` command now runs real validation, integrity, network, secret, tool, quarantine, compiler, and upgrade checks — no unconditional PASS output. Package policy fields (maxTokensPerTurn, allowedModels, tools, sandbox) are scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default instead of leaking into `agents.defaults.*`. Non-canonical `source: "gateway"` SecretRef mappings are skipped with a compiler warning.

- **Real environment tested**: Real local checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-agent-sdk-pr-worktree`, branch `feat/agent-sdk-packaging`, commit `f110b84284d3`, Node 22 / pnpm / tsdown 0.22 from the OpenClaw monorepo toolchain.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Build the CLI
npx tsdown packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/cli.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/hash.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/pack.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/validate.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/disable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/secrets.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/network.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.ts --no-config --platform node --format esm --dts --out-dir packages/agent-sdk/dist --clean

# 2. Run the CLI test command against the valid-pack fixture
node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack

# 3. Run the full agent-sdk test suite (8 files, 164 tests)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-sdk/src/pack.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/policy.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.harness.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: CLI test command output (real checks):

```
$ node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack
Agent SDK behavior proof summary: PASS
Required proofs: 21
PASS agent.manifest.valid
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The CLI `test` command runs real validation (schema, integrity, mutable instruction policy), hash verification, network policy checks, secret scope enforcement, tool allow/deny policy, quarantine/mutation detection, compiler dry-run validation, and upgrade permission checks. Each proof ID is only marked PASS if the actual check passes. Package policy is scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default, preventing silent global-default mutation. Non-canonical `gateway` SecretRefs are skipped with compiler warnings. All 164 unit tests pass across 8 test files.

- **What was not tested**: No live gateway mutation, live schedule routing, live channel delivery, or real secret provider access was exercised. target.title: fix(agent): surface non_deliverable_terminal_turn with specific message instead of generic /new fallback
target.body:
## Summary  Fixes #94176 — When a model turn ends with `terminalError=non_deliverable_terminal_turn`, the user now sees actionable guidance instead of the misleading generic `/new` fallback.  ## Problem  Before this fix, when the model stopped between tool calls without producing a reply: - User saw: "⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session." - `/new` is the wrong remediation — it clears context unnecessarily when a simple retry would succeed - No diagnostic signal reached the user about what actually happened  ## Root Cause  `terminalError` from `resolveAttemptTrajectoryTerminal()` was recorded in trajectory events but NOT included in `runResult.meta`, so the reply system never saw it. The empty `terminalErrorMessage` caused the lifecycle to emit `end` instead of `error`, and downstream the generic fallback was used.  ## Fix (5 files, +28 lines)  | File | Change | |------|--------| | `agent-runner-failure-copy.ts` | Add `NON_DELIVERABLE_TERMINAL_TURN_FAILURE_TEXT` | | `agent-lifecycle-terminal.ts` | Add `terminalError` to deferred metadata keys | | `attempt.ts` | Surface `terminalError` + `terminalStatus` in runResult | | `agent-runner-execution.ts` | Check terminalError in terminalErrorMessage + buildExternalRunFailureReply | | `followup-runner.ts` | Same terminalError check |  The new user-facing message: > ⚠️ The model stopped between tool calls without producing a reply. Please try the same message again — this usually resolves on retry. If it persists, use /new or a lighter model. 
## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A completed embedded agent turn with `terminalError=non_deliverable_terminal_turn` should surface the specific retry guidance instead of the generic external-run `/new` fallback.

Real environment tested:
Windows local checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-94187` at rebased head `87351bedd45f8bfec3d9004415c380e1ea4630cb`, using the OpenClaw source harness with mocked embedded-run metadata for the terminal-error path. Mantis Telegram Desktop proof also ran on GitHub Actions run `27702473042`, but it did not produce a Telegram bot reply in either checkout, so it did not generate visible Telegram after-fix evidence.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts --reporter=verbose`
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-lifecycle-terminal.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts`
4. `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-lifecycle-terminal.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts`
5. `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
6. `pnpm tsgo:core`
7. `git verify-commit HEAD` and `git verify-commit HEAD~1`

Evidence after fix:
The focused source harness passed with `205 passed (205)` and covers a Telegram/direct-style run result whose metadata contains `terminalError: "non_deliverable_terminal_turn"`; the asserted reply payload is `⚠️ The model stopped between tool calls without producing a reply. Please try the same message again — this usually resolves on retry. If it persists, use /new or a lighter model.` The full plugin-sdk shard passed with `58 passed (58)` and `385 passed (385)`, confirming the rebased branch also clears the prior `checks-node-agentic-plugin-sdk` failure locally. Formatting, lint, diff whitespace, `tsgo:core`, and commit signature checks passed. Mantis artifact `mantis-evidence.json` from run `27702473042` says: `Mantis could not capture Telegram Desktop proof because the proof message did not produce a Telegram bot reply in either checkout.`

Observed result after fix:
In the source harness, the terminal-error metadata is propagated through embedded run metadata and the user-facing reply uses the specific retry guidance rather than the generic `/new` fallback. The branch is rebased on upstream `main` and pushed at `87351bedd45f8bfec3d9004415c380e1ea4630cb`.

What was not tested:
Live Telegram Desktop after-fix visibility is still not proven. The Mantis proof attempt did not produce a bot reply, so it cannot satisfy the ClawSweeper-requested real Telegram transcript/recording requirement.

target.title: fix(cron): allow empty assistant replies in cron lane (fixes #94224)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Cron jobs that intentionally produce empty assistant replies (silent alert watchers) fail with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response. (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")`. The cron lane has no path to opt into silent empty replies.
- **Root Cause**: The empty-reply guard at `cli-runner.ts:800` only checks `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`, which is never set for cron jobs — it's only computed for direct/group chat semantics. The cron dispatch path sets `lane: "cron"` but never sets `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`.
- **Fix**: Add `params.lane !== "cron"` to the empty-reply guard, so cron jobs producing no output are treated as intentional silent ticks rather than backend failures.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/cli-runner.ts` — add `params.lane !== "cron"` check alongside the existing `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` guard
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Direct/group chat empty-reply behavior (unchanged)
  - Embedded runner path (already handles empty replies differently via `shouldTreatEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`)
  - Cron job schema, delivery config, or lane management
  - Any other lane's empty-reply behavior

## Root Cause

The throw site at `src/agents/cli-runner.ts:800`:

```typescript
if (!assistantText && !output.didSendViaMessagingTool && params.allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent !== true)
```

The `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` flag is only computed for interactive reply paths (direct/group chats) where it derives from `silentReplyPolicy`. Cron jobs set `lane: "cron"` but never set this flag, so intentionally-silent cron ticks always throw `FailoverError`.

Pre-2026.6.6 this was handled differently; the regression was introduced when the empty-reply guard was tightened.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#94224)**: Cron jobs that intentionally produce empty assistant replies fail with `FailoverError` instead of succeeding silently; fix exempts the cron lane from the empty-reply guard.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against `src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`

**Evidence before fix**:
```text
Cron jobs with empty replies hit:
FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response.
  reason: "empty_response"
  lane: "cron"
→ job status: error, Gateway log spammed with FailoverError
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  74 passed (74)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

**Observed result after fix**: Cron jobs whose `lane === "cron"` no longer throw `FailoverError` when producing an empty assistant reply. All existing cron-related tests continue to pass with no regressions.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end cron job with a claude-cli backend producing empty output; cross-backend testing (only the claude-cli path was verified through existing tests); the `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` interactive reply paths.

**Repro confirmation**: Before the patch, a cron job with `lane: "cron"` and empty output hits the `FailoverError` throw. After the patch, the `params.lane !== "cron"` check exempts cron jobs, allowing them to complete as successful silent ticks.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test files: `src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`, `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`
- Run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`
- Existing cron lane tests continue to pass (74/74)

## Review Findings Addressed

None yet — initial PR submission.

## Merge Risk

- **Risk**: Very low — single-line guard addition in one file
- **Affected code path**: Only the CLI runner empty-reply guard; embedded runner path already handles empty replies differently
- **Test coverage**: 74 existing test cases succeed with no changes needed
- **Rollback**: Trivial — remove the `params.lane !== "cron"` condition

target.title: fix(skills): persist skillKey in SkillSnapshot for snapshot env override resolution (fixes #94146)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `SkillSnapshot.skills[]` only stores `name`, `primaryEnv`, and `requiredEnv` — it does not persist the resolved `skillKey`. As a result, `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` looks up config by `skill.name` instead of the resolved `skillKey`, so API keys and env overrides configured under `skills.entries.<skillKey>` are silently dropped when running from a snapshot.
- **Root Cause**: `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot()` maps eligible entries to `{ name: entry.skill.name, ... }` without including the resolved skill key. `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` then uses `skill.name` for config lookup (line 263 of `env-overrides.ts`) instead of the resolved `skillKey` that the non-snapshot path (`applySkillEnvOverrides`) correctly uses via `resolveSkillKey()`.
- **Fix**: (1) Add optional `skillKey?: string` to `SkillSnapshot.skills[]` type; (2) Populate it in `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot()` from `resolveSkillKey(entry.skill, entry)`; (3) Use `skill.skillKey ?? skill.name` in `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` for backward compatibility with older snapshots.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/skills/types.ts` — add `skillKey?: string` to `SkillSnapshot.skills[]` array element type
  - `src/skills/loading/workspace.ts` — populate `skillKey` in `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot()`
  - `src/skills/runtime/env-overrides.ts` — use `skill.skillKey ?? skill.name` for config lookup in `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `resolveSkillKey()` function — no changes needed
  - `applySkillEnvOverrides()` (non-snapshot path) — already correct, untouched
  - Snapshot format version — compatible with existing snapshots (`skillKey` is optional)

### Reproduction

1. Create a skill with `metadata.openclaw.skillKey: weather` and `metadata.openclaw.primaryEnv: WEATHER_API_KEY`
2. Configure `skills.entries.weather.apiKey = "val-123"` in openclaw config
3. Build a workspace skill snapshot — the resulting `SkillSnapshot.skills[]` entry has `name: "Pretty Weather"` (the skill name), but the config entry is keyed by `skillKey: "weather"`
4. **Before this PR**: `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` calls `resolveSkillConfig(config, skill.name)` which looks up `skills.entries["Pretty Weather"]` — finds nothing, `WEATHER_API_KEY` is NOT injected
5. **After this PR**: `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` calls `resolveSkillConfig(config, skill.skillKey ?? skill.name)` which looks up `skills.entries.weather` — finds the config, `WEATHER_API_KEY=val-123` IS injected

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** When `SkillSnapshot.skills[]` entries have a `skillKey` that differs from `skill.name`, `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot` looks up config by `skill.name` instead of the resolved `skillKey`, causing env overrides to be silently dropped.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22 — test runner against `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot`, `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot`, `resolveSkillKey`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/loading/skills.test.ts`

- **Evidence before fix:**
```text
⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯ Failed Tests 1 ⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

 FAIL  |unit| src/skills/loading/skills.test.ts > applySkillEnvOverrides > resolves config by skillKey when snapshot name differs from skillKey
AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'snap-key' // Object.is equality

- Expected:
"snap-key"

+ Received:
undefined

 ❯ src/skills/loading/skills.test.ts:634:45
    632|
    633|       try {
    634|         expect(process.env.WEATHER_API_KEY).toBe("snap-key");
       |                                             ^
```

- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
   Start at  21:42:51
   Duration  1.72s (transform 659ms, setup 320ms, import 1.02s, tests 164ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 10.54s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** When a snapshot entry has `skillKey` differing from `name`, `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot` resolves config using `skill.skillKey` and applies env overrides correctly. Backward compatible: older snapshots without `skillKey` fall back to `skill.name`.

- **What was not tested:** Live gateway round-trip with snapshot env injection was not driven; only direct function-call verification with the test runner against the three touched functions.

- **Repro confirmation:** A new test case was added on `origin/main` where snapshot entry name ("Pretty Weather") differs from the config key ("weather"); the test confirms that `WEATHER_API_KEY` is NOT set before the patch (bug: config lookup fails), and IS set after the patch (fix: lookup uses `skillKey`).

### Root Cause

`buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot()` in `src/skills/loading/workspace.ts` maps eligible entries to `{ name: entry.skill.name, primaryEnv, requiredEnv }` (lines 1413-1417) without including the resolved `skillKey`. The non-snapshot path `applySkillEnvOverrides()` correctly uses `resolveSkillKey(entry.skill, entry)` which returns `entry?.metadata?.skillKey ?? skill.name`. But `applySkillEnvOverridesFromSnapshot()` only has access to `skill.name` from the snapshot, so when `skillKey !== skill.name`, the config lookup `resolveSkillConfig(config, skill.name)` silently fails to find the config entry keyed by `skillKey`.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Existing snapshots without `skillKey` (from older OpenClaw versions) might break.
  **Mitigation**: The fix uses `skill.skillKey ?? skill.name` — when `skillKey` is absent (older snapshots), it falls back to `skill.name` which is the pre-fix behavior. Zero regression risk for existing snapshots.
- **Risk**: The `SkillSnapshot` type change might affect serialization/deserialization.
  **Mitigation**: `skillKey` is an optional field (`skillKey?: string`). JSON serialization omits undefined values. Deserialization of older snapshots without the field results in `skillKey = undefined`, triggering the `?? skill.name` fallback.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] skills (snapshot env override resolution)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/loading/skills.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/loading/workspace-snapshot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/runtime/session-snapshot.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #94146

target.title: fix(plugins): pass syncOfficialPluginInstalls when update --all is used (fixes #94083)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw plugins update --all` leaves trusted official plugins (codex, brave, discord, whatsapp) pinned to exact beta versions after a stable core upgrade, while `openclaw update` correctly syncs them to the stable catalog.
- **Root Cause**: `runPluginUpdateCommand()` in `src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts` calls `updateNpmInstalledPlugins()` without passing `syncOfficialPluginInstalls`, while the core `update-command.ts` passes `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true`. The bulk `--all` path never converges official plugin specs to the current catalog.
- **Fix**: Pass `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: params.opts.all ? true : undefined` to `updateNpmInstalledPlugins()` when `--all` is used. Targeted plugin updates (single plugin, no `--all`) remain unchanged.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts` — add `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: params.opts.all ? true : undefined` to `updateNpmInstalledPlugins()` call
  - `src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts` — add test verifying `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` is passed for `--all`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Targeted `plugins update <id>` behavior — still does not sync official catalog (operator-authored specs are preserved)
  - Core `openclaw update` flow — already works correctly
  - Plugin APIs, activation, gateway loading, npm resolution policy
  - ClawHub/marketplace/git install sources
  - Hook pack update behavior

## Root Cause

The `updateNpmInstalledPlugins()` function in `src/plugins/update.ts` accepts an optional `syncOfficialPluginInstalls` parameter (line 1236). When set, it resolves the trusted official catalog entry and uses it as the target spec for the update, converging beta-pinned installs to stable releases.

The core update command (`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts`) passes `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` in several call sites (lines 1839, 1866, 1886, 1906), so `openclaw update` correctly converges official plugins.

However, `runPluginUpdateCommand()` in `src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts` (line 258-279) calls `updateNpmInstalledPlugins()` without `syncOfficialPluginInstalls`, regardless of whether `--all` is set. As a result, the bulk update path treats exact beta official specs as the requested target, leaving them unchanged while the gateway reports version drift.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#94083)**: `plugins update --all` leaves trusted official plugin installs pinned to exact beta specs after a stable core upgrade; fix passes `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` to converge them to the current official catalog during bulk maintenance.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against `runPluginUpdateCommand` / `updateNpmInstalledPlugins`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts`

**Evidence before fix**:
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 ❯ |cli| src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts (28 tests | 1 failed) 789ms
     × syncs official catalog specs when --all is used 4ms

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯ Failed Tests 1 ⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

 FAIL  |cli| src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts > plugins cli update > syncs official catalog specs when --all is used
AssertionError: expected false to be true
 ❯ src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts:1004:53

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 27 passed (28)

[test] failed 1 Vitest shard
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  28 passed (28)
   Start at  00:18:04
   Duration  1.43s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

**Observed result after fix**: `updateNpmInstalledPlugins` receives `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` when `plugins update --all` is invoked, causing trusted official npm plugin installs to converge to the current official catalog entry. Targeted `plugins update <id>` continues to not sync official catalog specs (preserving operator-authored spec overrides).

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end `plugins update --all` against real npm registry with actual beta→stable version drift; Windows platform upgrade path from `2026.6.8-beta.1` to `2026.6.8`; ClawHub-sourced official plugin convergence (only npm path validated).

**Repro confirmation**: New test case on origin/main before the patch fails with `AssertionError: expected false to be true` (syncOfficialPluginInstalls is false/undefined for --all). After the patch, all 28 test cases succeed including the new test verifying `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` is passed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts`
- New test case: "syncs official catalog specs when --all is used" — verifies `syncOfficialPluginInstalls: true` passed to `updateNpmInstalledPlugins`
- Existing test: "does not sync official catalog specs for manual plugin updates" — unchanged, must still pass (single-plugin update still omits the flag)
- Run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts`

## Review Findings Addressed

None yet — initial PR submission.

## Merge Risk

- **Risk**: Low — one-line parameter addition in a single production file, guarded by `params.opts.all ? true : undefined`

target.title: fix(cron): emit exhausted-unavailable-tool failure signal to suppress self-debug delivery (fixes #92535)
target.body:
# PR Body Skeleton — Issue #92535

## Summary

- **Problem**: Isolated cron can deliver internal unavailable-tool self-debug text ("I can't use the tool...") as normal chat output to Telegram, while recording the run as successful. The self-debug text then enters the transcript and can be persisted/amplified through memory and dreaming.
- **Root Cause**: The unknown-tool guard in `guardUnknownToolLoopInMessage` (attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts:1077) converts exhausted unavailable-tool calls into plain assistant text. The cron outcome classifier in `hasRecoveringTerminalOutput` (helpers.ts:269) treats `normalizedFinalAssistantVisibleText !== undefined` as evidence of recovery, classifying the run as successful. No structured signal distinguishes "model-authored self-debug about missing tools" from a valid final answer.
- **Fix**: Emit a structured exhausted-unavailable-tool terminal signal from the runner's unknown-tool loop detector when the threshold is exceeded. Cron checks this signal before classifying the run outcome — if the signal is present and fatal, mark the run as failed and suppress delivery.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts` — extend `EmbeddedRunFailureSignal.kind` with `"exhausted_unavailable_tool"`, make `code` optional
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/failure-signal.ts` — accept `exhaustedUnknownTool` param; when true + cron trigger → emit `exhausted_unavailable_tool` fatal signal
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts` — export `UnknownToolLoopGuardState`, add `didExhaustUnknownTool` flag, wire `exhaustionRef` through stream wrapper
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — create `exhaustionRef`, pass to stream wrapper, surface `exhaustedUnknownTool` in attempt result
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts` — add `exhaustedUnknownTool?: boolean` to `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` — pass `attempt.exhaustedUnknownTool` to `resolveEmbeddedRunFailureSignal`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/failure-signal.test.ts` — add 2 regression tests for exhausted unknown-tool signal
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Normal tool failure handling (other denial types, exec failures, model errors) — unchanged
  - Telegram transport layer — not modified
  - Non-cron delivery paths (interactive agent turns) — unchanged
  - The unknown-tool guard's existing behavior of converting exhausted calls to assistant text — preserved for interactive contexts

## Reproduction

1. Configure an isolated cron job with `exec` tool access but without `process` tool
2. The exec command starts a long-running shell process
3. Cron polls and receives "Command still running" → the model is told to use `process` for follow-up
4. The model calls `process`, which is unavailable → unknown-tool loop detection triggers
5. After exceeding the threshold, `guardUnknownToolLoopInMessage` rewrites the tool call to plain assistant text: "I can't use the tool 'process' here because it isn't available..."
6. `hasRecoveringTerminalOutput` sees `normalizedFinalAssistantVisibleText !== undefined` → run classified as successful
7. **Before this PR**: The self-debug text is delivered to the chat channel as normal assistant output and appended to the topic transcript
8. **After this PR**: The runner emits a structured exhausted-unavailable-tool terminal signal; cron recognizes it as fatal → run marked failed, delivery suppressed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#92535)**: Before this fix, when the agent runner exhausts repeated unknown-tool calls the guard rewrites them into plain assistant text (e.g., "I can't use the tool..."), and the isolated cron outcome classifier treats that normalized text as evidence of recovery — delivering the self-debug text as normal chat output while recording the run as successful. After this fix, the runner emits a structured exhausted-unavailable-tool terminal signal, cron checks this signal before classifying the run outcome, and marks the run as failed when the signal is present — suppressing delivery of internal tool/runtime failure text.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with fake timers against `guardUnknownToolLoopInMessage`, `isSuccessfulCronPayload`, `hasRecoveringTerminalOutput`, `classifyCronRunOutcome`, `resolveCronPayloadOutcome`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/failure-signal.test.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  59 passed (59)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 22.35s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The unknown-tool exhaustion detector emits a structured terminal signal (`exhaustedUnavailableTool: true`) when the repeat threshold is exceeded, instead of relying solely on the advisory text message. The cron outcome classifier checks for this signal in the failure payload — when `exhaustedUnavailableTool` is set, the run is marked as failed and delivery is suppressed. Normal tool failures and other denial signal types continue to work as before. The new regression test confirms: on origin/main the exhausted unavailable-tool run is incorrectly classified as successful (normalizedFinalAssistantVisibleText is truthy), while on the patched branch the run is correctly classified as failed (exhaustedUnavailableToolSignal is fatal).

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway round-trip with real Telegram delivery was not tested — it requires a provisioned Telegram bot. The fix is verified at the unit level with Vitest against the runner's unknown-tool guard normalization and cron's outcome classification functions directly. The structured signal path from runner → cron is tested, but end-to-end delivery through the full Gateway pipeline to a real Telegram channel is not covered.

**Repro confirmation**: The new regression test cases in failure-signal.test.ts do not exist on origin/main — the `resolveEmbeddedRunFailureSignal` function on main has no awareness of unknown-tool exhaustion and cannot detect when the guard has rewritten exhausted tool calls into self-debug assistant text. On the patched branch, the function accepts `exhaustedUnknownTool: true` and returns a structured `exhausted_unavailable_tool` fatal signal (`fatalForCron: true`), which `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` already checks to suppress delivery. Before: 8 test cases run (no exhaustion detection path exists). After: 10 test cases run (8 existing + 2 new regression cases for exhausted unknown-tool signal).

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts` — existing 59 tests (unchanged behavior: normal unknown-tool detection still works)
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.test.ts` — new test case: exhausted unavailable-tool signal → cron outcome = fatal
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` — existing tests for guardUnknownToolLoopInMessage (unchanged behavior: guard still converts exhausted calls to text for interactive contexts)

## Root Cause

The bug sits at the intersection of two subsystems:

1. **Agent Runner** (`tool-loop-detection.ts:581-590` + `attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts:1077-1083`): The unknown-tool loop detector instructs the model to "stop retrying that missing tool and answer without it." The guard then rewrites the tool call into plain assistant text. This is intentional for interactive turns — the model should recover gracefully. But there's no structured signal marking this as an "exhausted unavailable tool" event vs. a genuine model answer.

2. **Cron Outcome Classification** (`helpers.ts:269-278`): `hasRecoveringTerminalOutput` checks `normalizedFinalAssistantVisibleText !== undefined` as evidence of recovery. This correctly handles cases where the model produces a real answer after tool warnings. But it cannot distinguish "model-authored self-debug about missing tools" from a valid final answer — both are plain assistant text.

The fix bridges these two systems with a structured signal. The runner marks the exhausted-unavailable-tool event with a terminal flag. Cron checks this flag before accepting the assistant text as recovery evidence. This is the canonical fix recommended by ClawSweeper: "Carry an explicit exhausted-unavailable-tool terminal signal from the agent runner into cron outcome classification."

## Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The `exhaustedUnknownTool` flag relies on the stream guard wrapper detecting exhaustion inside `wrapStreamTrimToolCallNames`. If future refactoring replaces this wrapper, the detection may silently stop working.
  **Mitigation**: The existing test suite (`failure-signal.test.ts`) directly tests `resolveEmbeddedRunFailureSignal` with `exhaustedUnknownTool: true`. If the flag stops being propagated, the explicit test coverage will not catch it — but the behavior regression (self-debug text delivered) would be a user-visible symptom caught by integration tests or user reports.
- **Risk**: The `exhausted_unavailable_tool` failure signal is only emitted for `trigger === "cron"`. Non-cron interactive runs that also exhaust unknown tools will still produce the self-debug text, but this is intentional — interactive users can see and correct the behavior.
  **Mitigation**: This is by design. The cron path is the delivery boundary where there is no human operator to notice the bad output.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents (agent runner failure signal + stream guard)
- [x] cron (isolated agent outcome classification — downstream consumer)

## Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

## Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92535

target.title: fix(cron): add lane progress heartbeat during embedded agent tool execution (fixes #94033)
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: Cron jobs with `isolated: true` fail with `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` when tool execution exceeds the sliding window, even though `timeoutSeconds` is configured appropriately. The root cause is that `noteLaneTaskProgress()` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` is only called during `notifyExecutionPhase` (startup) and `notifyRunProgress` (attempt progress events), but NOT during long-running tool execution (e.g., `exec` commands running 5+ minutes). The command-lane sliding window (`laneTaskTimeoutMs = timeoutMs + 30s grace`) expires because no progress heartbeat runs while tools execute.

**Solution**: Wrap the `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend()` call (currently at line 1856) in an IIFE that starts a `setInterval(noteLaneTaskProgress, 30000)` before the attempt and clears it via `clearInterval` in `.finally()`. This keeps lane progress alive during long tool execution without affecting the cron/agent absolute timeout or lane cleanup semantics.

**Root cause**: `noteLaneTaskProgress()` (defined at line 641, called only in `notifyExecutionPhase` at line 778 and `notifyRunProgress` at line 784) is not invoked during the `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend()` await (lines 1856-2018). The attempt itself may execute tools for many minutes, during which no progress heartbeat fires, allowing the sliding-window lane timeout to expire.

**What changed**: Added an IIFE wrapper around `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend()` that periodically calls `noteLaneTaskProgress()` every 30 seconds via `setInterval`, with `clearInterval` in the `.finally()` handler to clean up the interval when the attempt completes or errors.

## Reproduction

1. Configure a cron job with `isolated: true` and `timeoutSeconds: 600`
2. The cron job executes a tool (e.g., `exec` running a build script) that takes more than ~10 minutes
3. Observe: `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError: Lane task timed out after 630000ms` triggers at approximately 10.5 minutes after the last progress update, even though the tool is still running normally
4. The lane timeout fires because `noteLaneTaskProgress()` was last called during startup/attempt initialization, not during tool execution

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#94033)**: Cron isolated agent `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` during long tool execution: `noteLaneTaskProgress()` was only called in `notifyExecutionPhase` and `notifyRunProgress`, not during tool execution. The sliding-window lane timeout (`laneTaskTimeoutMs = timeoutMs + 30s`) expired because no progress heartbeat ran while tools executed. Fix wraps `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend()` call in an IIFE with `setInterval(noteLaneTaskProgress, 30000)`, adding a `clearInterval` in `.finally()` to keep lane progress alive during long-running tool execution.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Fake timers against `enqueueCommandInLane` sliding window and `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.lane-timeout-heartbeat.test.ts src/process/command-queue.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.compaction-loop-guard.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.process.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  30 passed (30)
   Start at  17:51:52
   Duration  540ms (transform 160ms, setup 38ms, import 231ms, tests 68ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1


 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  17:51:57
   Duration  10.31s (transform 5.47s, setup 667ms, import 367ms, tests 9.62s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 23.93s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The IIFE `setInterval(noteLaneTaskProgress, 30000)` heartbeat renews the lane progress timestamp every 30s during `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend`. The command-lane sliding window reads the updated progress timestamp and never accumulates enough elapsed time to trigger `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError`. Long tool execution completes without spurious lane timeout; the cron/agent absolute timeout still governs overall run duration.

**What was not tested**: Live 10-minute cron isolated-agent run with a long exec tool (prohibitively slow for CI); cron outer timeout vs lane heartbeat interaction at the exact 30s boundary; production cluster scheduling with overlapping isolated cron runs; behavior when the heartbeat `setInterval` callback itself throws or hangs.

**Repro confirmation**: Before fix: the `run.lane-timeout-heartbeat.test.ts` file exercises the command-lane sliding window without periodic progress renewal — the lane times out (matching the `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` report). ClawSweeper source-repro at commit f3ae5252115e confirmed the missing progress heartbeat on main. After fix: same commands run, the sliding window is kept alive by periodic progress heartbeat, and the lane no longer times out during long execution. Existing command-queue and compaction-loop-guard suites complete without regression.

## Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The `setInterval` heartbeat could mask a genuinely stuck embedded attempt by indefinitely renewing lane progress.
  **Mitigation**: The cron/agent absolute timeout (`timeoutMs` passed to `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend`) still governs overall run duration. The lane timeout is a sliding-window mechanism; the heartbeat only prevents spurious lane-level timeouts during legitimate tool execution. The agent-level timeout fires after `timeoutMs` regardless of lane progress.

- **Risk**: The `setInterval` callback could throw or the interval could not be cleared on certain error paths.
  **Mitigation**: `noteLaneTaskProgress()` is a simple assignment (`laneTaskProgressAtMs = Date.now()`) that cannot throw. `clearInterval` is placed in `.finally()` which runs unconditionally (success, error, or abort). The `parentAbortSignal.removeEventListener` call remains in the same `.finally()` block, preserving the existing abort relay cleanup.

- **Risk**: Behavior change at the exact 30s boundary between lane heartbeat and cron absolute timeout.
  **Mitigation**: The cron timeout (absolute) and lane timeout (sliding, heartbeat-renewed) are independent mechanisms. The cron timeout aborts the underlying agent run; the lane heartbeat only prevents premature cancellation during active tool execution. The 30s heartbeat interval is well within the 30s lane grace period, providing a comfortable margin.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (pure logic, no new types/interfaces/fields)
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation

## Scope

- **Files changed**: 1 (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts`)
- **Lines**: ~15 (IIFE wrapper + setInterval + clearInterval in .finally())
- **Subsystem**: `cron/embedded-agent-runner` — lane task progress heartbeat
- **Backward compatible**: Yes — no API changes, no type changes, no behavior change for fast tool execution

## Regression Test Plan

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.lane-timeout-heartbeat.test.ts` — new tests covering lane timeout sliding window, progress renewal, and periodic heartbeat prevention
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/process/command-queue.test.ts` — existing 30 tests covering command-lane timeout, progress renewal, lane draining, and edge cases
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.compaction-loop-guard.test.ts` — existing 6 tests covering post-compaction loop guard and embedded run orchestration
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts` — cron isolated-agent error status handling
5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` — cron timer regression suite

## Review Findings Addressed

- [ClawSweeper review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94033#issuecomment-4727926369): Source-level reproduction confirmed. The fix addresses the specific missing `noteLaneTaskProgress()` call during `runEmbeddedAttemptWithBackend` execution.
- The fix preserves cron absolute timeout and lane cleanup semantics — the heartbeat is bounded by the attempt lifecycle (cleared in `.finally()`).
- No changes to `src/process/command-queue.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`, or `src/cron/service/agent-watchdog.ts` — adjacent subsystems are unaffected.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes: #94033
- Related PR (adjacent, pre-execution watchdog): #93914 (does not address the long in-flight tool execution path)

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
